title: Auxiliary laps-to-next-stop target with censored multi-task training
summary: A recent F1 pit-stop paper reports a cascaded setup where next-lap classification is complemented by estimating remaining laps to stop, and race-strategy literature treats pit timing as a dynamic timing problem rather than a pure binary event.
rationale: A recent F1 pit-stop paper reports a cascaded setup where next-lap classification is complemented by estimating remaining laps to stop, and race-strategy literature treats pit timing as a dynamic timing problem rather than a pure binary event. This could sharpen ordering near the decision boundary better than adding more fixed thresholds.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary target `laps_to_next_pit` within each `Race-Year-Driver` sequence, censoring rows with no later stop in the session. Train a shared tabular model with two heads or two-stage stack: head A predicts `PitNextLap`, head B predicts `laps_to_next_pit` / stop-within-k-horizon probabilities. Feed OOF horizon predictions back into the final classifier.
expected_effect: Better discrimination for borderline laps where raw tyre age is ambiguous, especially late-stint and late-race rows.
risk: Requires careful censored-label construction and leakage-safe sequence ordering; noisy auxiliary labels may hurt if the stop horizon is too sparse.
